title: Resilience Patterns
summary: Circuit breaker, bulkhead, retry, fallback, and timeout patterns with Resilience4j in Spring Boot.

sections.content:
## Why Resilience?

In microservices, every remote call can fail. Without resilience patterns, one slow downstream service can take down your entire application through thread exhaustion and cascading failures.

**Resilience4j** is the successor to Netflix Hystrix for Spring Boot applications.

sections.title: Resilience4j Decorator Chain
sections.content:
graph LR
    Call[Service Call] --> RL[RateLimiter]
    RL --> B[Bulkhead]
    B --> CB[CircuitBreaker]
    CB --> Retry[Retry]
    Retry --> TO[TimeLimiter]
    TO --> Svc[Downstream Service]
    style CB fill:#4f46e5,color:#fff
    style B fill:#059669,color:#fff

## Bulkhead Pattern

Isolate resources so one slow service can't starve others:

```java
@Bulkhead(name = "inventoryService", type = Bulkhead.Type.THREADPOOL)
public CompletableFuture<Stock> checkStock(String productId) {
    return CompletableFuture.supplyAsync(() ->
        inventoryClient.getStock(productId));
}
```

```yaml
resilience4j:
  thread-pool-bulkhead:
    instances:
      inventoryService:
        maxThreadPoolSize: 10
        coreThreadPoolSize: 5
        queueCapacity: 20        # reject if queue full → bulkhead rejects
```

Now even if `inventoryService` is completely slow:
- Maximum 10 threads blocked on it
- All other services still have their full thread pools
- `BulkheadFullException` thrown immediately when queue full

sections.title: Resilience Pattern Decision Tree
sections.content:
graph TD
    Problem[Service Call Problem] --> Q1{Is service\njust slow?}
    Q1 -->|Yes| Timeout[Apply Timeout\n+ TimeLimiter]
    Q1 -->|No, failing| Q2{Is it transient\n(network blip)?}
    Q2 -->|Yes| Retry[Apply Retry\nwith backoff]
    Q2 -->|No, consistently failing| CB[Apply CircuitBreaker\nfast-fail + fallback]
    Q1 & Q2 --> Q3{Affecting\nother services?}
    Q3 -->|Yes| Bulkhead[Apply Bulkhead\nisolate thread pool]
    style CB fill:#4f46e5,color:#fff
    style Timeout fill:#d97706,color:#fff
    style Retry fill:#059669,color:#fff

sections.question: What happens when a CircuitBreaker transitions to OPEN state?
options: All calls are queued until the service recovers
Calls immediately fail fast (throw exception or call fallback) without calling the downstream service
Calls are retried with exponential backoff
The thread pool is expanded to handle backlog
sections.explanation: When the circuit is OPEN, calls immediately fail fast — the downstream service is not called at all. This prevents thread pile-up on a known-bad service and allows it time to recover. After the configured wait time, the circuit transitions to HALF-OPEN and allows a probe request through.
